summary: MCP process.env wholesale delegation — all host env vars passed to child processes
detail: mcp/index.ts:456 passes ...process.env to StdioClientTransport, exposing all host environment variables (including secrets, tokens, API keys) to every MCP child process. This is the broadest trust boundary violation in the codebase. Should pass only mcp.environment + safe allowlist.

summary: Hook dispatch guard capability enforcement bypassed via as-any casts
detail: plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts:57-59 uses 'as any' casts on CapabilityId values, bypassing enum type safety. A mistyped capability string in the hook map would silently pass type checking and potentially grant unintended permissions.

summary: MCP auth data silently discarded on corruption via Schema.decodeUnknownOption fallback
detail: mcp/auth.ts:62 uses Option.getOrElse with a fallback to empty object when auth file decoding fails. Corrupted or tampered auth files result in all stored OAuth tokens silently lost with no error/log/notification.

summary: Plugin identity optional throughout dispatch pipeline — ListedPlugin has pluginId?
detail: plugin/index.ts:82 defines ListedPlugin as 'Hooks & { pluginId?: string }' making pluginId optional. Capability checks depend on pluginId for security state lookup. Legacy plugins without an id bypass identity-based security.

summary: Plugin dynamic import executes arbitrary code without sandbox
detail: plugin/loader.ts:141 loads third-party code via dynamic import() in the same process. Module shape typed as Record<string, unknown> with only weak typeof guards. No process isolation, no capability preflight, no Worker sandbox.

summary: [P0] Empty-string pluginId from resolvePluginId failure contaminates all security operations
detail: When resolvePluginId fails for an external plugin, Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed('')) silently produces empty-string identity. Multiple plugins share this id, contaminating capability checks, dispatch guards, crash tracking, and quarantine. A plugin without a real id can inherit another plugin's security state.

summary: [P0] Positional hooks/registrations array binding enables cross-plugin capability contamination
detail: hooks[] and registrations[] are parallel arrays bound only by index. They're populated at different lifecycle phases. hooks.length is used as loop bound but registrations[i] is optional-accessed. Out-of-sync arrays cause capability checks to authorize the wrong plugin's hooks.
